title: boenninghausia vincentina
intro: Boenninghausia vincentina, also known as the Piquiá, is a plant species native to the Amazon region, which belongs to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Boenninghausia vincentina include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and astringent. Boenninghausia vincentina is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its leaves can also be used in herbal remedies to treat various health issues.
